ext a
  -- We need to show that 1/5 * abs (9 + 2 * a) < 1 iff a is in the interval (-7, -2)
  simp only [Rat.cast_inv₀, Rat.cast_one, Rat.cast_mul, sSupNorm.good_reconstruct, mul_sub, one_mul]
  -- First, assume that a is in the interval (-7, -2)
  constructor
  intro h
  -- Normalize the inequality: Divide by 5 and transpose terms to get it on one side
  . apply abs_btwn
    rw [← sub_pos]
    { norm_num }
    { ray_tac }
  intro h
  -- Use normNum to confirm numerically feasible
  norm_num

    norm_num
  · intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨a, b, hb, hab, rfl⟩
    have hb' : 0 < b := by linarith
    have ha : 0 < a := by linarith
    have ha' : 0 < a / b := by positivity
    have hb'' : 0 < b / a := by positivity
    have h : Real.logb a (a / b) + Real.logb b (b / a) ≥ 0 := by
      rw [Real.logb, Real.logb, div_eq_mul_inv, Real.log_mul (by positivity) (by positivity), Real.log_inv]
      have h1 : Real.log a / Real.log b ≥ 0 := by
        apply div_nonneg
        exact Real.log_nonneg (by linarith)
        exact Real.log_nonneg (by linarith)
      have h2 : Real.log b / Real.log a ≥ 0 := by
        apply div_nonneg
        exact Real.log_nonneg (by linarith)
        exact Real.log_nonneg (by linarith)
      linarith
    linarith

  have h2 : Real.logb b (b / a) = Real.log (b / a) / Real.log b := by simp [Real.logb]
  have h3 : Real.log (a / b) = Real.log a - Real.log b := by
    rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
  have h4 : Real.log (b / a) = Real.log b - Real.log a := by
    rw [Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
  rw [h1, h2, h3, h4]
  have h5 : Real.log a ≠ 0 := by
    rw [← Real.log_one]
    apply Real.log_lt_log
    all_goals linarith
  have h6 : Real.log b ≠ 0 := by
    rw [← Real.log_one]

    all_goals linarith
